chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Hastelloy process density meter and viscometer for highly corrosive applications

Modified on: Tue, 10 Mar, 2026 at 4:58 PM

[Image of a Rheonics sensor probe with 'HASTELLOY C-22' label, and puzzle pieces representing Nickel (Ni) and Molybdenum (Mo) with atomic numbers and masses]

What is the purpose of this article?
Describe Rheonics sensor probes with Hastelloy C22 wetted parts for applications with highly corrosive fluids, material characteristics, and use cases.

What products are involved?
(https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/)Rheonics inline SRV viscometer (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/),
and density and viscosity meter SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/).

Hastelloy C22 Material

The Hastelloy C22, or simply Alloy C22, is known for being a corrosion-resistant material, composed mainly of nickel, chromium, and molybdenum.

The Alloy C22 is used for a wide range of harsh chemicals in the industry including:

*   Chloride fluids (e.g. Calcium, ferric, magnesium, aluminum chloride)
*   Nitric acid - HNO3
*   Sulfuric acid - H2SO4
*   Hydrochloric acid - HCl
*   Oxidizing acid, etc.

Some key characteristics of the Hastelloy C22 material are:

*   Composition: The nickel-chromium-molybdenum composition of Hastelloy C22 has an overall better performance and resistance than other Hastelloy variants (e.g. C276, C4, 625).
*   Corrosion resistance: Used in severe corrosive fluids (presence of acids, chloride, nitric, oxidizing fluids, etc.) even at high temperatures.
*   Application: Used in piping and pumping systems, general industry, automotive, chemical, etc. where exposure to corrosive fluids restricts the use of alternative metals (e.g. Stainless Stel 316, 316L, 904L)
key_facts: Rheonics offers sensor probes with Hastelloy C22 wetted parts for applications involving highly corrosive fluids.
Hastelloy C22, also known as Alloy C22, is a corrosion-resistant material primarily composed of nickel, chromium, and molybdenum.

Alloy C22 is suitable for a wide range of harsh industrial chemicals, including chloride fluids (e.g., Calcium, ferric, magnesium, aluminum chloride), nitric acid (HNO3), sulfuric acid (H2SO4), hydrochloric acid (HCl), and oxidizing acids.
The nickel-chromium-molybdenum composition of Hastelloy C22 provides superior performance and resistance compared to other Hastelloy variants like C276, C4, and 625.
Hastelloy C22 maintains its corrosion resistance in severe corrosive fluids, including acids, chlorides, nitric, and oxidizing fluids, even at high temperatures.

Hastelloy C22 is applied in piping and pumping systems across various industries such as general industry, automotive, and chemical, particularly where corrosive fluids restrict the use of alternative metals like Stainless Steel 316, 316L, and 904L.
The document provides contact information: Phone: +1 713 234 0863.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the material characteristics of Hastelloy C22, its application in Rheonics inline density and viscosity sensors for corrosive environments, and its superior chemical compatibility compared to Stainless Steel 316L.
product_names: Rheonics SRD Inline process Density and Viscosity meter
chunks.extracted_text:
Table 1: Hastelloy C22 Material Characteristics

| Name | Hastelloy C22 Material |
| :------------------------------ | :--------------------- |
| | Hastelloy C22 |
| | DIN 2.4602 |
| | NiCr21Mo14W |
| Composition | Nickel Ni 50-63% |
| | Chromium Cr 20 - 23% |
| | Molybdenum Mo 12 - 14% |
| | Others |
| PREN (Pitting Resistance Equivalent Number) | 46 |
| Density | 8.61 g/cm3 |

Rheonics Sensors with Hastelloy C22 wetted surface

Rheonics SRD Inline process Density and Viscosity meter, and SRV Inline process Viscometer are available with Hastelloy C22 material as probe's wetted part or fluid exposed area. This means that the material in contact with the fluid is Hastelloy C22, compatible for corrosive fluids and various acids. The back side of the probe, including the connector, is made of Stainless Steel 316L (SS 316L) standard material, as shown in Figure 2.

Figure 2: C22 Probe with SS316 back side.Wetted parts are shaded in light blue.

The Hastelloy C22 probe variants enables the use of Rheonics inline sensors to measure density, viscosity and temperature, as well as related parameters such as concentration, of corrosive fluids and acids even at high temperatures without affecting the sensor operation, integrity and accuracy. This results in constant monitoring and tight control of the fluid's properties.

Figure 3: SRV and SRD Long Insertion NPT 1.25" (SR-X5-Axxx-B1N25)

Rheonics Hastelloy C22 high corrosion resistance viscosity and density sensor probes are recommended when Stainless Steel 316L (1.4435) - the standard sensor probe's material - is not compatible with the application fluids.

Table 2: Benefit of Hastelloy over stainless for corrosive environments

Compatibility of Hastelloy and Stainless Steel with corrosive fluids

| Chemical Fluid | Stainless Steel 316L (1.4435) | Hastelloy C22 (2.4602) |
| :---------------------- | :------------------------------ | :--------------------- |
| Aluminum chloride | Bad | Good |
| Ammonium chloride | Bad | Good |
| Calcium chloride | Bad | Good |
| Calcium nitrate | Bad | Good |
| Chloric acid | Bad | Good |
| Ferric chloride | Bad | Good |
| Hydrochloric acid | Bad | Good |
| Sulfuric acid | Bad | Good |
key_facts: Rheonics SRD and SRV inline sensors are available with Hastelloy C22 as the wetted part for contact with corrosive fluids and various acids.
The non-wetted parts of the probe, including the connector, are made of Stainless Steel 316L.

Hastelloy C22 probe variants allow Rheonics inline sensors to measure density, viscosity, and temperature, as well as related parameters like concentration, in corrosive fluids and acids even at high temperatures without compromising sensor operation, integrity, or accuracy.
Hastelloy C22 high corrosion resistance viscosity and density sensor probes are recommended when Stainless Steel 316L (1.4435) is not compatible with the application fluids.

Hastelloy C22 demonstrates 'Good' compatibility with Aluminum chloride, Ammonium chloride, Calcium chloride, Calcium nitrate, Chloric acid, Ferric chloride, Hydrochloric acid, and Sulfuric acid, whereas Stainless Steel 316L is 'Bad' for all these chemical fluids.
warnings_notes: Stainless Steel 316L (1.4435) - the standard sensor probe's material - is not compatible with the application fluids for which Hastelloy C22 is recommended.

chunks.page_summary: This page details Rheonics Type-SR sensor variants made with Hastelloy C22, outlining their applications across various industries and providing critical installation guidelines, including sensor probe dimensions and required clearances.
product_names: Rheonics SRD Density and Viscosity Meter
chunks.extracted_text:
Chemicals compatibility with Stainless Steel 316L, Hastelloy C22 and Titanium Gr. 5.
(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412247-process-viscometer-and-density-meter-chemical-compatibility)
The Rheonics sensor probes offered with Hastelloy C22 wetted material are shown in next table.

Table 3: Rheonics sensor variants offered with Hastelloy C22
| Sensor Code | Sensor variant with Hastelloy C22 |
|---|---|
| SR-C22-X1 | Type-SR Sensor short probe with threaded connection, e.g. G1/2, 3/4" NPT |
| SR-C22-X2 | Type-SR Sensor short probe with custom connection, e.g. Flanges |
| SR-C22-X3 | Type-SR Sensor short probe with Tri-Clamp connection |
| SR-C22-X4 | Type-SR Sensor flush probe with custom connection, e.g. Tri-Clamp, Flanges |
| SR-C22-X5 | Type-SR Sensor long insertion probe with custom connection, e.g. Flange, API Hub, Tri-Clamp |

Applications of Inline Density meter and Viscometer with Hastelloy C22
Some industries and applications where Rheonics Type-SR sensors made of Hastelloy C22 are:
*   Automotive industry: Density monitoring and acid concentration (e.g. sulfuric acid) on battery performance and production.
*   Brine, potassium chloride and water, and hydrochloric acid and hypochlorous acid.
*   Chemical Processing and Science: Brine for electrochemical cell with hypochlorous acid.
*   Chemical crystallization.
*   Pharmaceutical: Ethanol distillation. Acids and cleaning density monitoring.
*   Pulp and paper - ammonium Sulfite Mill.
*   Refinery Industry - coke refinery at vaporizer.
*   Water and wastewater treatment.

Installation of Rheonics Hastelloy C22 Density meter and Viscometers for corrosive fluids
Rheonics Type-SR sensors have a simple installation process. The main requirement is to have the sensing area of the sensor probes correctly immersed in fluid without dead zones as shown in next image.

Rheonics SRV Viscometer
SRV tip's clearance to any obstruction 5 mm [0.2"]

Rheonics SRD Density and Viscosity Meter
SRD tip's clearance to any obstruction 12 mm [0.5"]

Avoid stagnation zones in the sensing zones (red areas)
Ensure sensing element in red is in contact with fluid
Figure 4: Type-SR sensor probe's sensing element.

For further installation considerations, visit next pages for each sensor:
*   SRD Density and Viscosity meter installation articles (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000297659)
*   SRV Viscometer installation articles (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000297658)

Rheonics optional accessories for installation (e.g. flow cells for installation in 1" lines, weldolets, protection cages, etc.) are mostly manufactured in Stainless Steel 316L and are not offered as standard in Hastelloy C22 material. Enquire with sales team for available options.
key_facts: Rheonics sensor probes are offered with Hastelloy C22 wetted material.
Rheonics Type-SR sensors are utilized in diverse applications, including automotive, chemical processing, pharmaceutical, pulp and paper, refinery, and water/wastewater treatment.
The primary requirement for installing Rheonics Type-SR sensors is to ensure the sensing area of the probes is correctly immersed in fluid without creating dead zones.
The sensing element, indicated by red areas in diagrams, must be in direct contact with the fluid.
Rheonics optional installation accessories (e.g., flow cells, weldolets, protection cages) are predominantly manufactured from Stainless Steel 316L and are not standard in Hastelloy C22 material.

Rheonics SRD Density and Viscosity Meter
SRD tip's clearance to any obstruction 12 mm [0.5"]
Figure 4: Type-SR sensor probe's sensing element.
warnings_notes: Avoid stagnation zones in the sensing zones (red areas).
Ensure sensing element in red is in contact with fluid.
For further installation considerations, visit the SRD Density and Viscosity meter installation articles and SRV Viscometer installation articles.
Rheonics optional accessories for installation are mostly manufactured in Stainless Steel 316L and are not offered as standard in Hastelloy C22 material.
Enquire with the sales team for available options regarding accessories.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides an ordering guide and configuration options for Rheonics SR-Type inline viscometers and density meters, detailing material choices, process connection types, and required information for quotation.
chunks.extracted_text:
Plastic materials e.g. PTFE can be used as accessories for compatibility with corrosive fluids.

How to order Rheonics viscometer and density meter made of Hastelloy C22 material?

How to order Rheonics Type-SR sensor with flange?

Code Form: SR_ - C22 - X_ - A___ - B______
Examples:
  SRV - C22 - X1 - 34N
  SRD - C22 - X5 - A200 - BD50CR

1 Rheonics SR- Type sensor
  SRD: Inline Density and Viscosity meter
  SRV: Inline Viscometer

2 Wetted material
  Available variants:
    C22: Hastelloy C22
    SS: Stainless Steel 316L

3 Process Connection Configuration
  Define the process connection from the options shown*. Probe variants compatible with C22 material are:
    -X1: Threaded probes.
    -X2: Short variant. Define flange with norm, size, pressure rating, etc.
    -X3: Tri Clamp probes
    -X4: Flush probes.
    -X5: Long insertion variant. Define flange with norm, size, pressure rating, etc.
  *Rheonics can keep adding new process connection variants.

4 Insertion Depth - "A" - If applicable
  Only applicable for -X5.
  The insertion length should be defined, this is the distance between the sensor tip and the flange. Probe wetted zone.

5 Process Connection "B"
  Process connection should be defined by the user for all flanges with norm, size, pressure rating, etc.
  Rheonics will then create the corresponding flange's code.
  Prefix "B" is only used for -X5 sensors.
  Examples:
    34N: 3/4” NPT
    BD50CR: DIN Flange DN50 PN16 RF for -X5

(https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/srd/)
SRV viscometer online RFQ
(https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/srv/)

Material
Sensor probe wetted part
  Stainless steel 316L (1.4435)
  C22: Hastelloy C22 (2.4602)

  X1 : 3/4" NPT threaded probe (Article)
  X1 : G 1/2" BSPP threaded probe (Article)
  X2 : Probe with Flange, specify flange DN/PN in box below (Article)

  X5 : Long insertion probe (FPC), select A & B in sections below (Article)
  X6 : Slimline probe (SLP), select A & B in sections below (Drawing)
  X7 : Reactor probe (RP), select L & B in sections below (Drawing)

For X2, X3, X4: Specify type, norm and size of process connection:

For X5, X6 and X7: Use the following fields to specify further configuration
A: Insertion Length - For X5 and X6, input insertion length of probe, A (in mm) in box below. Specify unit if other than mm.

L: Total Probe Length - For X7

B: Process connection type, norm and size - For X5, X6 and X7

For X2, X3, X4: Specify type, norm and size of process connection:

For X5, X6 and X7: Use the following fields to specify further configuration
A: Insertion Length - For X5 and X6, input insertion length of probe, A (in mm) in box below. Specify unit if other than mm.

Complete the RFQ (https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/) with missing details (viscosity and/or density range, calibration accuracy, temperature rating, etc.), submit the form, and the Sales Team will contact you soon.

specifications.Process Connection B Example 2: BD50CR: DIN Flange DN50 PN16 RF

key_facts: Plastic materials like PTFE can be used as accessories for compatibility with corrosive fluids.
The Rheonics SR-Type sensor can be configured as an SRD (Inline Density and Viscosity meter) or an SRV (Inline Viscometer).
Available wetted materials for the sensor probe include Hastelloy C22 (2.4602) and Stainless Steel 316L (1.4435).

Process connection configurations include threaded probes (X1), short variant flanges (X2), Tri-Clamp probes (X3), flush probes (X4), long insertion probes (X5), slimline probes (X6), and reactor probes (X7).
Insertion depth 'A' is only applicable for -X5 and -X6 probes and defines the distance between the sensor tip and the flange/wetted zone.
Process connection 'B' specifies the flange type, norm, size, and pressure rating, and is used for -X5, -X6 and -X7 sensors.
To complete an order, an online Request For Quotation (RFQ) form must be submitted with details such as viscosity/density range, calibration accuracy, and temperature rating.
Rheonics will create the corresponding flange code based on user-defined process connection 'B' details.
The prefix 'B' for process connection is only used for -X5, -X6, and -X7 sensors.
